findings.title: Durable shared and practice profiles encode firm operating context

evidence.description: Directs user configuration to durable paths, migrates it across plugin updates, and shares company facts across twelve plugins.
Requires ordinary practice-area answers to apply configured jurisdiction, risk posture, playbook positions, and escalation chain.
States that the practice profile improves through use and may record new positions.

source_to_sink: typed answers and expressly supplied seed documents
subsequent skills and plugins read the shared/practice profile
findings.proves: The static design deliberately persists reusable firm context and uses it across later workflows, supporting a customer-specific professional interaction graph at the local configuration layer.
findings.does_not_prove: It does not establish provider-side retention, provider access to the files, cross-customer pooling, training, or product improvement.
findings.alternative_explanation: Durable local configuration is the expected benign mechanism for consistent customer-specific assistance.
findings.test_to_confirm: Trace runtime file access and network egress while creating, editing, and using these profiles; inspect contractual custody and retention terms.

findings.title: Employment setup captures substantive judgment signals from answers and prior work product

evidence.description: Explains that risk calibration and house conventions are written to a reusable profile, limits inputs to typed or approved material, and disclaims ambient personal-history collection.
Offers to build positions, stances, fallbacks, review triggers, and escalation points from user input or existing playbooks.
Requests employment documents and extracts house format, severance posture, high-risk patterns, and actual approval patterns.

source_to_sink: user answers and explicitly supplied employment documents
future hiring, termination, policy, and escalation workflows
findings.proves: The repository explicitly solicits and structures firm-specific positions, fallbacks, escalation criteria, house style, and observed approval patterns.
findings.does_not_prove: It does not show that the extracted information leaves customer custody or is reused by Anthropic beyond serving that customer.
findings.alternative_explanation: The captured signals are necessary to avoid generic, jurisdiction-insensitive legal workflow output.
findings.test_to_confirm: Observe prompts, tool calls, file writes, and outbound requests during cold start and later profile-driven reviews; compare against tenant data controls.

findings.title: Litigation intake creates a structured, longitudinal matter and decision record

evidence.description: Captures identification, conflicts, risk, owners, legal hold, dates, and posture into matter, history, and portfolio-log files with confirmation.
Preserves conflicts findings, waivers, owners, due dates, and bypass rationale as continuing records.
Captures the initial fight, settle, investigate, or wait posture.
Defines the matter log as the durable rollup source of truth with searchable closed-matter history.

source_to_sink: matter facts and user conflict/risk judgments
portfolio rollups and later matter updates
findings.proves: The static workflow builds an explicit longitudinal graph of matter identity, conflicts, posture, owners, rationale, and outcomes inside local matter storage.
findings.does_not_prove: It does not prove the proprietary runtime transmits that graph to a provider or derives generalized litigation strategy from it.
findings.alternative_explanation: A durable matter ledger is ordinary legal-operations recordkeeping and improves auditability and consistency.
findings.test_to_confirm: Instrument intake and portfolio-status runs for local reads/writes and egress; inspect access controls and deletion behavior for closed matters.

findings.title: Configured connectors and scheduled agents create bounded remote source and notification paths

evidence.description: Declares remote Slack and Google Drive MCP endpoints for workspace messages and documents.
Defines a scheduled VDR watcher with wildcard Box, Intralinks, Datasite, and Slack-send tools.
Describes daily VDR queries and posting deal updates, with local-file fallback when integrations are unavailable.
Limits the watcher to flagging new documents and reporting checklist status rather than reading documents or updating the checklist.

source_to_sink: VDR metadata and configured deal state
Slack deal channel or local update file
findings.proves: The design includes remote connector calls and an ambient scheduled notification path; the watcher tool declaration is broader than its stated read-only/document-flagging behavior.
findings.does_not_prove: It does not prove connector activation, tenant permissions, actual payloads, provider logging, retention, training, or covert exfiltration.
findings.alternative_explanation: Remote reads and team notifications are explicit user-facing workflow functions, with documented local fallbacks and stated limits.
findings.test_to_confirm: Capture runtime MCP authorization, exact tool calls and payloads, Slack destinations, credential scopes, logs, and retention during a scheduled run.

findings.title: Managed-agent cookbooks support hosted deployment while isolating untrusted documents from write and network authority

evidence.description: Describes headless fleet review for POST /v1/agents and repeatedly bounds extracted cells as unverified leads.
Separates untrusted-document readers from write holders and connectors and requires schema/length controls and spreadsheet-injection defenses.
Requires deployment-time connector/output wiring and states the agent never directly uploads or posts to Slack.
Treats document bytes as data and makes the extractor read-only with no MCP, network, or write authority.

findings.proves: The repository contemplates hosted managed-agent execution but includes explicit authority separation that materially weighs against a simple document-to-exfiltration chain.
findings.does_not_prove: Static cookbook controls do not prove the deployed service enforces them, nor do they answer service-side logging, retention, model inference, or secondary use.
findings.alternative_explanation: The architecture is an affirmative least-authority design intended to safely automate ordinary diligence.
findings.test_to_confirm: Deploy in a controlled tenant and verify effective tool grants, blocked outbound calls, schema caps, write isolation, service logs, and operator access.

findings.title: Default cross-matter isolation coexists with deliberate practice-level learning

evidence.description: Sets cross-matter context off, prohibits reading another matter by default, and routes approved reusable learnings to the practice profile.
Repeats default matter isolation and the practice-level destination for cross-matter learnings.
Defines client/matter workspaces as an isolation mechanism for multi-client practice.

findings.proves: The design distinguishes prohibited automatic cross-matter reads from a controlled channel for carrying generalized firm learnings across matters.
findings.does_not_prove: It does not prove runtime enforcement, prevent a user from manually moving sensitive material, or establish any cross-customer transfer.
findings.alternative_explanation: This is an explicit privacy and privilege control paired with a normal mechanism for maintaining firm-wide standards.
findings.test_to_confirm: Use canary data in two matters to verify negative reads, then test the exact approval and provenance required to promote a learning into the practice profile.

findings.title: Product launch workflow separates privileged reasoning from tracker-safe output and gates clearance

evidence.description: Requires explicit attorney-review confirmation before affirmative launch clearance for non-lawyer users.
Requires separate privileged and redacted outputs and forbids rationale, citations, and escalation detail in the tracker block.
Directs users to post only the redacted block and restrict the full analysis to the privilege circle.

findings.proves: The static instructions recognize destination-sensitive disclosure and attempt to prevent legal rationale from flowing into broadly shared trackers.
findings.does_not_prove: It does not prove perfect redaction, tracker connector enforcement, user compliance, or provider-side confidentiality and retention.
findings.alternative_explanation: The split-output pattern is a substantive safeguard that supports benign workflow automation rather than covert collection.
findings.test_to_confirm: Run adversarial launch examples and compare both outputs for rationale leakage; verify any tracker connector receives only the redacted artifact.

new_patterns.name: Durable professional profile promotion
new_patterns.logic: Find instructions that extract customer-specific positions or approval patterns into version-independent shared or practice configuration consumed by later workflows.
new_patterns.false_positive_risk: A durable local profile establishes customer adaptation, not provider custody or secondary use.

new_patterns.name: Matter isolation with controlled abstraction
new_patterns.logic: Pair default cross-matter denial with any explicit mechanism for promoting reusable matter learnings into a practice-level store.
new_patterns.false_positive_risk: Human-approved firm-level standardization is not cross-customer pooling.

new_patterns.name: Ambient connector path with stated behavioral limit
new_patterns.logic: Compare scheduled-agent tool grants and wildcard connectors against prose limits, fallbacks, and actual output destinations.
new_patterns.false_positive_risk: Declared tools may be unavailable or more constrained at runtime; prose may not be enforcement.

new_patterns.name: Privilege-boundary transform
new_patterns.logic: Locate workflows that derive a shareable artifact from privileged analysis and test whether rationale or sensitive metadata can cross the boundary.
new_patterns.false_positive_risk: The existence of a shareable output does not show sensitive content actually leaks.

negative_evidence.description: Limits setup inputs to typed answers and explicitly uploaded documents and requires approval before using visible conversational context.
negative_evidence.effect: Weighs against ambient or undisclosed personal-history harvesting in the documented design.

negative_evidence.description: Sets cross-matter context off by default and says a skill in matter A never reads matter B.
negative_evidence.effect: Contradicts an unbounded automatic cross-matter collection theory at the static-instruction layer.

negative_evidence.description: Separates untrusted readers, connectors, and writers; direct upload and Slack posting are excluded from the agent.
negative_evidence.effect: Materially weakens a direct document-to-arbitrary-egress source-to-sink theory for this cookbook.

negative_evidence.description: States the agent does not send Slack itself and the cookbook does not schedule itself.
negative_evidence.effect: Bounds ambient authority and makes orchestration/deployment configuration an observable proof gap.

negative_evidence.description: Requires attorney-review gating and separate tracker-safe output stripped of legal rationale.
negative_evidence.effect: Shows affirmative human and destination controls inconsistent with an indiscriminate disclosure design.

negative_evidence.description: Says the watcher flags documents without reading them and reports checklist status without updating it.
negative_evidence.effect: Limits the stated scheduled-agent collection and mutation surface, subject to runtime verification.

unknowns.question: Do profile, matter, prompt, tool, or connector contents leave customer-controlled storage during hosted inference?
unknowns.why_source_cannot_answer: The repository provides static prompts and paths but not proprietary runtime network traces or service architecture.
unknowns.needed_evidence: Tenant-scoped packet/tool-call captures, service architecture, operator-access controls, and authenticated runtime logs.

unknowns.question: What telemetry, support, abuse-monitoring, and diagnostic payloads are collected from these workflows?
unknowns.why_source_cannot_answer: No effective hosted telemetry implementation or production event schema is present in the assigned source.
unknowns.needed_evidence: Production telemetry schemas, sampled events, destination mapping, retention schedules, and contractual disclosures.

unknowns.question: Are customer-specific workflow signals retained or used for generalized model or product improvement?
unknowns.why_source_cannot_answer: Static inference and local persistence instructions contain no source-to-sink evidence for retention plus secondary use.
unknowns.needed_evidence: Current contracts and product terms, training/reuse policy, opt-in state, data lineage, deletion evidence, and auditable backend controls.

unknowns.question: Are cross-matter isolation, read-only MCP scopes, and managed-agent tool tiers enforced by the deployed runtime?
unknowns.why_source_cannot_answer: Prompt declarations and cookbook manifests describe intended behavior but are not runtime enforcement evidence.
unknowns.needed_evidence: Effective authorization manifests, negative canary tests, tool-call logs, sandbox policy, and deployment configuration.

unknowns.question: Who operates each external MCP endpoint and what are its retention and downstream-use rules?
unknowns.why_source_cannot_answer: The repository names endpoints and capabilities but not authenticated tenant terms or operator-side processing.
unknowns.needed_evidence: Connector-specific DPA/terms, subprocessor identity, scopes, retention, audit logs, and deletion tests.

unknowns.question: Does the redacted tracker workflow reliably prevent privileged rationale leakage under adversarial inputs?
unknowns.why_source_cannot_answer: The source specifies the transformation but contains no executed output corpus or leakage evaluation.
unknowns.needed_evidence: Adversarial fixtures, paired privileged/redacted outputs, automated leakage checks, and human legal review.
